title: Error Boundary
description: Implement a basic catch mechanism to prevent the entire mobile app from crashing on a JS error.
problemStatement:
Fail safely! Given a component that might crash, wrap its usage in a `try...catch` block (simulated via logic) or describe how you would use a third-party Error Boundary.

**Requirement:** Provide a fallback UI message `"Something went wrong"`.
hints: React Native doesn't have a built-in functional Error Boundary, but you can use `componentDidCatch` in class components or specialized libraries.
expectedOutput: App remains functional with an error message instead of a 'Red Screen of Death'
